summary.quantification_method: Relative abundance based on area under the curve (AUC) of extracted ion chromatograms

matched_class_path: gene modification or gain of function
primary_variables.matched_definition: Base or amino-acid substitution (for example SNV, point mutation, engineered missense).
evidence: we selected nine NOTCH1 variants that could cause a change in O-fucosylation of key EGFs
Mass spectral glycoproteomic site mapping was used to identify alterations in O-fucosylation of EGFs containing the mutations.
point mutations in N1 can lead to alterations in O-fucosylation that deregulate the Notch pathway

matched_class_path: gene modification or gain of function
primary_variables.matched_definition: Addition of bases or sequence cassette (for example donor insertion, tag insertion, transposon insertion).
evidence: The N386T mutation introduced a neo O-fucosylation site in the ligand-binding domain of the N1 receptor

attributes.agent: NOTCH1 variants (G230R, G309R, G310R, T311P, G347S, T349P, N386T, D464N, A465T)
evidence: we selected nine NOTCH1 variants that could cause a change in O-fucosylation of key EGFs
We also expressed and purified a N1 fragment containing all the EGFs in question (EGF5 to EGF14) in Chinese hamster ovary (CHO) cells
expressed and purified a N1 fragment... in the presence or the absence of LFNG for mass spectral glycoproteomic analysis

replicate_hints: All experiments were performed in biological triplicates or more

evidence: we compared them with WT N1 using cell-based Notch activation and ligand-binding assays
expressed and purified a N1 fragment... in the presence or the absence of LFNG for mass spectral glycoproteomic analysis

quantification_strategy: Relative abundance based on area under the curve (AUC) of extracted ion chromatograms

base_candidates_ranked.reason: Data was acquired on a Q-Exactive Plus Orbitrap using HCD on top 10 precursor ions (DDA) and quantified via area under the curve (AUC) of extracted ion chromatograms (LFQ-like relative abundance).

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment is explicitly described as 'mass spectral glycoproteomic site mapping' focusing on O-fucosylation and its extensions.
